task:
Round 4+5 combined — bug fixes from live deploy + points overhaul. PLAN AS 1 STAGE.

PART A — Bug fixes (9 issues observed on live deploy):

1. Edit mode auto-closes when clicking description input. When user clicks pencil → edit mode opens, but clicking inside the description textarea closes the edit mode. The click-outside handler treats the textarea as "outside" — likely the textarea is rendered outside cardRef container, OR there's a stopPropagation missing. Fix: ensure all editable fields (rename input, description textarea, priority cycler, archive button) are inside the cardRef so click-outside doesn't fire. If they are inside, check if a re-render is unmounting/remounting the ref. Stage.tsx and TaskCard both have this issue.

2. Avatars in HomeView are not pressable. The TeamBar / workspace summary card avatars don't open user popups. Should reuse the same interactive avatar pattern (with onClick that opens user profile popup) used in Stage.tsx / pipeline list. Make HomeView avatars use the same `onUserClick` handler and visual hover state.

3. Archive button position — currently stacked inside the description area in card edit mode. Move to bottom-left of the card edit-mode panel, separated from the description textarea. Both Stage.tsx (stageEditMode panel) and TaskCard (editOpen panel).

4. Emoji reactions sometimes roll back. User clicks emoji on stage or comment, optimistic toggle shows the change, then it reverts. Investigate: does `handleReact`/`handleCommentReact` rollback on server 200? Check pushCommentReaction and the regular pushReaction paths. Likely cause: the rollback fires on ANY non-ok response, including 304 or non-error responses.

5. Duplicated "assign" buttons. Top-right of card has "assign" pill + action row also has "assign" with popover. Remove the top-right "assign" pill — action row is the canonical place. Same for Stage.tsx and TaskCard.

6. Archive on task says "archived" but task stays visible AND archive view is empty. Trace: archiveStage handler updates archivedStages state ✓, schedules write ✓, but the kanban/list filter that should hide archived stages is not picking up the change. Check: does TasksView filter `allStages.filter(s => !archivedStages.includes(s))`? Does Stage.tsx's PipelinesView render archived stages? And ArchiveView reads `archived.stages` — make sure that's actually populated.

7. Upload (image) button overlaps with pencil button. The upload button (gallery/image upload) is in the same bottom-right corner as the pencil. Move upload to the LEFT of the pencil with a small gap, or to bottom-left of the card.

8. Description click-to-edit must be removed. Currently in Stage.tsx and PipelinesView.tsx (pipeline-level description), clicking the description text directly puts it into edit mode — but the user wants ALL editing to be gated behind the pencil button (Notion-style). Audit:
   - Stage.tsx: description click-to-edit on stage description → remove, only edit when stageEditMode=true
   - PipelinesView.tsx: pipeline description click-to-edit → remove, only edit when pipelineEditMode === pipelineId
   - Pipeline name click-to-edit (if any) → same
   - TaskCard: title click was already converted (rename only via pencil) — verify still
   - Subtask cards: same audit
9. Claim button visual emphasis. Currently "+ claim" button is small and de-emphasized. Make it more visible: brighter accent color background tint, larger font size or weight, potentially a subtle pulse on hover. The trophy moment must FEEL like an opportunity. Don't break the ClaimChip component contract; just adjust the unclaimed variant style.

PART B — Points-only system with dynamic sum + LLM suggestions:

10. Drop "hours" concept entirely. All references in code: stageDefaults `hours` field, pipeline `totalHours` derived, sharePipeline output, etc. Audit and remove. Don't break existing data — read code can ignore hours fields, but no UI surfaces them.

11. Dynamic point sums:
- Pipeline points = sum of all its stages' points (derived per render, not stored). Use useMemo.
- Stage points = sum of subtask points if any non-archived subtasks exist, else uses stage's own points field. Derived per render.
- Subtask points = leaf-level, stored as `subtaskPoints: Record<SubtaskKey, number>` or directly on the SubtaskItem (cleaner — add `points?: number` field).

12. User override of derived points:
- When a stage's points are derived from subtasks, the user can still EXPLICITLY override that stage's points via edit mode. Store override in `stagePointsOverride: Record<stageId, number>`.
- When override is present, derived value is suppressed.
- A small "↶ revert to suggested (X pts)" button appears next to the override field, clicking removes the override.

13. LLM-suggested points endpoint. New file: `src/app/api/suggest-points/route.ts`.
- POST body: `{ kind: "pipeline" | "stage" | "subtask", title: string, context?: string }`
- Use OpenAI (already in deps via /api/chat route) with model gpt-4o-mini (cheap + fast). Reuse OPENAI_API_KEY env var pattern. Match the response_format json_object pattern if available.
- System prompt: "You are estimating effort for a pipeline/stage/subtask in a project tool. Reply ONLY with a JSON object: {points: <integer 1-50>, rationale: <one short sentence>}. Points should reflect typical effort: 1-3 for trivial, 5-13 for medium, 20-50 for major."
- User prompt: pass title + optional context
- Rate-limited (10/min/IP)
- Session-required (getServerSession check)
- Return parsed JSON or 400 if Claude's response can't be parsed.

14. UI: in edit mode of stage/subtask/pipeline, when points field is empty or first opened, show a "✨ suggest" button next to it. Click → calls /api/suggest-points with title and gets a number. Field shows the suggestion as placeholder text grayed out. User can submit (accepts suggestion) or type a number.

15. The "use suggested" revert button mentioned in #12 — wire it. When override exists and `dynamicSum > 0` (subtasks present), show "revert to {dynamicSum}". When override exists and no subtasks, show "revert to suggested" — which re-fetches LLM suggestion.

CONSTRAINTS:
- TypeScript clean: npx tsc --noEmit after EACH commit
- Single STAGE but multiple commits is fine
- All bug fixes must verify regression-free (no new bugs introduced)
- LLM endpoint is rate-limited + auth-gated — security review
- Data shape changes: prefer adding fields over breaking ones (add `points?` to SubtaskItem, add `stagePointsOverride` slice; don't migrate existing data)
- Voice consistency: any new copy uses lowercase irreverent style ("// suggest", not "Suggest")
- No new external deps unless absolutely required (OpenAI SDK is already used in src/app/api/chat/route.ts — reuse that pattern)

stages.spec:
# Round 4+5 — Bug fixes (9) + Points overhaul (6). Single stage, multiple commits OK.

## Why this will be good
Edit mode finally feels like Notion (clicks inside don't kill the panel; pencil is the only edit gate). Avatars are pressable everywhere — no more dead pixels. Reactions never roll back the user's intent. Archive actually archives. The "+ claim" button reads as an opportunity, not a footnote. The points system becomes dynamic — pipeline = sum of stages, stage = sum of subtasks (overridable), with an LLM `// suggest` button so users never face a blank field. Hours are gone (they were never tracked accurately anyway). Production-ready: every new field round-trips through PATCH whitelist, every API call is auth-gated and rate-limited, all derivations are memoised so the leaderboard math doesn't churn on every render.

## Order of work — do bug fixes first, then points overhaul
Bug fixes are local edits to existing components; points overhaul is a data-shape change that touches the model context, validators, types, and several views. Doing bugs first keeps each commit focused and makes regression testing simpler.

## File-by-file plan

### PART A — Bug fixes

**Bug 1 — Edit mode auto-closes on textarea click**
Files: `src/components/Stage.tsx`, `src/components/TasksView.tsx` (TaskCard ~L478, EditableSubtaskCard ~L744)
Root cause hypothesis: the click-outside handler reads `cardRef.current` but some editable elements (textarea, priority cycler, archive button) render in a portal or are conditionally re-mounted, breaking ref containment. Or the handler fires on `pointerdown` and any DOM update during the same tick re-evaluates ref.
Required fix:
- Audit every edit-mode element in both Stage.tsx (search for `stageEditMode`) and TaskCard (search for `editOpen`). Confirm rename input, description textarea, priority cycler, archive button, points input, and any popovers (assign popover) are children of the element bound to `cardRef`.
- If anything is rendered via portal (e.g. floating popover for assignment), exclude its DOM node from the click-outside check using a separate `popoverRef` that's also tested in the contains() guard, OR move the portal element to be a sibling DOM node owned by the same outer ref.
- Switch the click-outside listener from `mousedown` to `pointerdown` only if it fixes the issue without side effects; otherwise keep `mousedown` and fix containment.
- Verify that re-renders during typing don't unmount/remount cardRef. If they do, hoist the ref to a stable parent.
Acceptance: open edit mode on stage card and on task card. Click into description textarea, type, click rename input, click priority cycler — edit mode stays open. Only clicking truly outside the card (e.g. on the page background) closes it.

**Bug 2 — HomeView avatars are not pressable**
Files: `src/components/HomeView.tsx`
The TeamBar / workspace summary avatars currently render `<AvatarC user={u} size={...} />` with no wrapping click handler. Make them open the user popup using the same pattern as Stage.tsx (which uses `onUserClick` prop already passed through ChromeShell — see `Stage.tsx` for callers, e.g. it threads through to a setState that opens a stats popup).
Required fix:
- Find where HomeView consumes the model. Wire an `onUserClick` callback (either from props or from the model context that owns the user popup state) and wrap each `<AvatarC>` in a `<button>` (no border) or `<div onClick>` with `cursor: pointer` and a subtle hover state (e.g. `transform: scale(1.05)` on hover or a `t.bgHover` background ring).
- Reuse whatever opens the user popup elsewhere — do not invent a new popup. Search for `setUserPopup`, `userStats`, or similar in ModelContext/Dashboard to find the canonical handler.
Acceptance: every avatar in HomeView is clickable and opens the user stats popup. Hover state is visible. Keyboard focus works (use a `<button>` element).

**Bug 3 — Archive button position in edit panel**
Files: `src/components/Stage.tsx`, `src/components/TasksView.tsx`
Currently the archive button sits inside or directly below the description textarea. Move it to bottom-left of the edit-mode panel, visually separated from the description block (own row, with a top border or `marginTop` and `paddingTop` separator). Description textarea takes the full width above; below it, a horizontal flex row: archive on the left, points field + suggest button on the right (this also lays the groundwork for Part B).
Acceptance: in stage edit mode and task card edit mode, archive button is in a dedicated row at the bottom-left of the panel. It does not visually associate with the description textarea.

**Bug 4 — Emoji reactions roll back**
Files: `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx` (handleReact), `src/lib/apiSync.ts` (pushReaction, pushCommentReaction). Possibly `src/components/Stage.tsx` for handleCommentReact.
Root cause hypothesis: rollback fires on any non-`res.ok` response. `res.ok` is true only for 200-299. A 304 Not Modified is `ok: true` so that's not it; more likely a 404/500 from the locked-pipeline 423 path or a timing race where the optimistic update is overwritten by the next `fetchState()` poll.
Required fix:
- Read `handleReact` and `handleCommentReact`. Confirm rollback condition. If it fires on `!res.ok`, narrow it: only roll back on 4xx/5xx that's NOT 423 (locked) — for 423, surface a "pipeline locked" toast instead of silently reverting.
- Check `fetchState()` polling cadence in ModelContext — if it overwrites the local optimistic reaction with stale server state, gate the merge: don't overwrite `reactions[stageKey]` if a local optimistic toggle is in flight (track via `pendingReactions: Set<string>` ref).
- Add a `console.warn` (temporary, remove before commit) to confirm the actual response code being seen.
Acceptance: clicking emoji on stage and on comment toggles persists across page reload. No flicker-back. If pipeline is locked, a toast says so and the toggle does roll back (correct behavior).

**Bug 5 — Duplicated assign buttons**
Files: `src/components/Stage.tsx`, `src/components/TasksView.tsx` (TaskCard top row ~L530-580)
Remove the top-right "assign" pill from both Stage card and TaskCard. The action row's assign button (with popover) is canonical. Search for `assign` text + anything that renders in the top-right cluster alongside priority/status.
Acceptance: only ONE assign control per card. Action row assign popover still works.

**Bug 6 — Archive doesn't hide the task; archive view stays empty**
Files: `src/components/TasksView.tsx`, `src/components/views/PipelinesView.tsx`, `src/components/Stage.tsx`, and the ArchiveView component (search for `ArchiveView` or `archivedStages` consumers in `src/components/views/`).
Required fix:
- Trace `archiveStage(stageName)` in ModelContext. Confirm it pushes to `archivedStages: string[]` AND `patchState({ archivedStages })`.
- In TasksView and PipelinesView, find every place that renders the list of stages. Filter out archived: `stages.filter(s => !archivedStages.includes(s))`. Currently this filter is missing or applied to a stale value.
- ArchiveView: verify it reads `archivedStages` from the model and renders them. If the path is `archived.stages`, that's wrong — should be `archivedStages` directly.
- Verify the archive toast/confirmation fires AFTER the state update, not before.
Acceptance: clicking archive on any stage immediately hides it from kanban, list, and pipeline views. The same stage appears in ArchiveView. Reload preserves archived state.

**Bug 7 — Upload button overlaps pencil**
Files: `src/components/Stage.tsx` (and TaskCard if it has upload — likely Stage only; verify).
Move the image upload button to the left of the pencil with a 6-8px gap, OR move it to the bottom-left of the card opposite the pencil. DEV chooses based on what looks cleanest in the rendered card. Don't prescribe pixels — just ensure no visual overlap at any card width down to 320px.
Acceptance: at 320px and at desktop widths, upload and pencil buttons are visually separate, both fully clickable.

**Bug 8 — Description click-to-edit must be removed (Notion-style: pencil is the only gate)**
Files: `src/components/Stage.tsx`, `src/components/views/PipelinesView.tsx`, `src/components/TasksView.tsx` (TaskCard + EditableSubtaskCard).
Required fix:
- Stage.tsx: stage description currently has an onClick that triggers edit mode. Remove that onClick. Description renders as plain text unless `stageEditMode === stageName`.
- PipelinesView.tsx: pipeline description has the same pattern. Remove the onClick on the description block. Description renders as plain text unless `pipelineEditMode === pipelineId`.
- Pipeline name click-to-edit: search for `pipeNameEdit` or similar — if it exists, remove it. Pencil only.
- TaskCard: confirm title click is already gated on edit mode. Don't re-introduce.
- Subtask cards (EditableSubtaskCard in TasksView): same audit. Subtask text should not be click-to-edit.
Acceptance: clicking on any description text or title text does NOTHING. Only clicking the pencil button enters edit mode. This must be true for: stage description, pipeline description, pipeline name, task title, subtask text.

**Bug 9 — Claim button visual emphasis**
Files: `src/components/ui/ClaimChip.tsx`
Adjust the unclaimed variant only (`claimed === false`). Current style is muted (border + textMuted). Make it feel like an opportunity:
- Background: `${pipelineColor}15` (8% tint) instead of transparent
- Border: `1px solid ${pipelineColor}66` instead of `t.border`
- Color: `pipelineColor` instead of `t.textMuted`
- Font weight: 700
- Slightly larger font (e.g. 11→12 for stage variant, 10→11 for subtask)
- Hover: bump background to `${pipelineColor}25`, optional `transform: scale(1.03)`
- The existing `pulse` prop animation must still work (don't break the claimAnim trigger)
Don't change the claimed variant — it already reads correctly.
Acceptance: side-by-side, the new "+ claim" button is visibly more prominent than the current one. The `pulse` prop still triggers a glow on top-claim stages. No regression in the claimed-state visual.

### PART B — Points overhaul

**Item 10 — Drop hours concept (audit-and-remove sweep)**
Files to audit:
- `src/lib/data.ts` — Pipeline interface has `totalHours: string`. Keep the field on the interface (don't break read of existing serialized state) but stop populating it for new custom pipelines. Better: mark it `totalHours?: string` and let callers stop reading it.
- `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx` — line 776 `totalHours: "?h"` when creating custom pipeline → remove this field assignment.
- `src/components/Dashboard.tsx` — `sharePipeline` (~L238) signature `(pid, pname, pdesc, priority, hours, stageList)` and the lines output that includes `${hours}` → remove `hours` parameter and replace with computed `${derivedPipelinePoints} pts`.
- `src/components/views/PipelinesView.tsx` — line 246 passes `p.totalHours` to sharePipeline; line 279 renders `{p.totalHours}` → both must be replaced with derived points display.
- `src/components/views/PipelinesView.tsx` PipelinesViewProps — sharePipeline signature update.
- `src/components/OverviewPanel.tsx` — line 10 type and line 358 renders `{p.totalHours}` → replace with derived points.
- `src/lib/generatePDF.ts` — line 155 outputs `${p.totalHours} · ${p.points}pts` → drop the hours half.
- `src/components/views/ChatView.tsx` — verify no hours references.
Important: do not remove `totalHours` from the Pipeline TYPE definition in data.ts and ModelContext.tsx — leave it as `totalHours?: string` for backward-compat of any localStorage entries. Just stop writing it and stop displaying it.
Acceptance: `git grep -i "totalHours\|hours " src/components src/lib` returns zero references inside JSX or display strings. Type definitions retain `totalHours?: string` for safety. No hours value is rendered in any view.

**Item 11 — Dynamic point sums (derived, not stored)**
Files: `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx`, `src/lib/data.ts`, `src/components/Stage.tsx`, `src/components/views/PipelinesView.tsx`, `src/components/OverviewPanel.tsx`, `src/components/TasksView.tsx`, `src/components/HomeView.tsx`.
Data shape (additive — no migration):
- `SubtaskItem` (in `src/lib/data.ts` line 334): add `points?: number` field. Default at render time: use `defaultSubtaskPoints` constant (define in data.ts as `export const DEFAULT_SUBTASK_POINTS = 5`).
- New ModelContext slice: `stagePointsOverride: Record<string, number>` — alongside `stageDescOverrides`. Add to: useState init from lsGet, lsSet useEffect persistence, fetchState merge, stagePatch object, useMemo deps array, exposed in context value, exposed in PATCH whitelist.
- DEV must export from ModelContext: `stagePointsOverride`, `setStagePointsOverride(stageId: string, pts: number | null)` — passing `null` removes the override.
- Add to `PATCH_KEY_WHITELIST` in `src/lib/validate.ts`: `"stagePointsOverride"`.
- Add to `apiSync.ts` `PatchBody` type: `stagePointsOverride?: Record<string, number>`.
- Add to `storage.ts` lsKey list (line 11 area).

Derivation helpers (define in `src/lib/data.ts` or a new `src/lib/points.ts`):
```ts
export const DEFAULT_SUBTASK_POINTS = 5;

export function deriveStagePoints(
  stageName: string,
  subtasks: SubtaskItem[] | undefined,
  archivedSubtaskKeys: Set<string>,
  stageDefaultPoints: number,
): number {
  const live = (subtasks || []).filter(s => !archivedSubtaskKeys.has(`${stageName}::${s.id}`));
  if (live.length === 0) return stageDefaultPoints;
  return live.reduce((sum, s) => sum + (s.points ?? DEFAULT_SUBTASK_POINTS), 0);
}

export function deriveStageDisplayPoints(
  stageName: string,
  subtasks: SubtaskItem[] | undefined,
  archivedSubtaskKeys: Set<string>,
  stageDefaultPoints: number,
  stagePointsOverride: Record<string, number>,
): number {
  return stagePointsOverride[stageName] ?? deriveStagePoints(stageName, subtasks, archivedSubtaskKeys, stageDefaultPoints);
}

export function derivePipelinePoints(
  stages: string[],
  archivedStages: string[],
  ...args needed for stage derivation
): number {
  // sum of deriveStageDisplayPoints for each non-archived stage
}
```
DEV can adjust signatures based on what's available in context — just keep the derivation pure.

Consumer changes (use `useMemo`):
- Stage.tsx: replace any `s.points` or `stageDefaults[name].points` display with `deriveStageDisplayPoints(...)` memoised on subtasks/override changes.
- PipelinesView.tsx: pipeline points display uses `derivePipelinePoints(...)` memoised. Remove direct read of `p.points`.
- OverviewPanel.tsx: leaderboard / pipeline metrics use derived values.
- HomeView.tsx: any pipeline summary uses derived values.
- TasksView.tsx: kanban/list cards show derived stage points.

`getPoints(userId)` in ModelContext (the "active stage = +pts" rule, line ~894+ area): UPDATE to use `deriveStageDisplayPoints` so claimers earn the dynamic value, not the static default. This is the leaderboard impact — make sure existing tests/QA don't regress.

Acceptance:
- Add a subtask with no explicit points → stage points increases by `DEFAULT_SUBTASK_POINTS` (5). Pipeline total increases by 5.
- Set a subtask's points to 20 → stage points reflects sum, pipeline reflects new total.
- Archive all subtasks of a stage → stage falls back to its `stageDefaults` value.
- All values reactive without page reload. No flicker. `useMemo` deps are correct (no infinite re-render loop).

**Item 12 — User override of derived stage points**
Files: `src/components/Stage.tsx` (stage edit panel), `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx`.
In stage edit mode panel, show a numeric input for points. Behavior:
- Display value: `stagePointsOverride[name] ?? derivedFromSubtasks` (if subtasks exist) `?? stageDefaults[name].points` (no subtasks).
- On user typing a number and committing (blur or Enter): call `setStagePointsOverride(name, parseInt(value))`.
- If user clears the field (empty string on blur): call `setStagePointsOverride(name, null)` — removes override.
- When override is present AND subtasks exist: render a small button next to the input — `↶ revert to {derivedFromSubtasks}`. Click → `setStagePointsOverride(name, null)`. Use `t.textMuted` color, font-size 10-11px, lowercase.
- When override is present AND no subtasks exist: render `↶ revert to suggested` — clicking re-fetches LLM suggestion (Item 15).
Acceptance: override persists across reload. Revert button removes the override and stage falls back to the dynamic sum. PATCH to MongoDB succeeds (whitelist includes `stagePointsOverride`).

**Item 13 — LLM-suggested points endpoint**
File: NEW `src/app/api/suggest-points/route.ts`.
Mirror the pattern from `src/app/api/chat/route.ts` (OpenAI fetch, AbortController 30s timeout, rate limit, logApi).
Required:
- Auth-gate via `getServerSession(authOptions)` — return 401 if no session.
- Rate-limit 10/min/IP via `rateLimit(req, "/api/suggest-points", 10, 60_000)`.
- Body validation: `{ kind: "pipeline" | "stage" | "subtask", title: string, context?: string }`. Validate `title` non-empty, ≤200 chars. Validate `context` ≤2000 chars if present. Validate `kind` is one of three values.
- Call OpenAI gpt-4o-mini with `response_format: { type: "json_object" }`.
- System prompt: `"You are estimating effort for a {kind} in a project tool. Reply ONLY with a JSON object: {\"points\": <integer 1-50>, \"rationale\": <one short sentence>}. Points should reflect typical effort: 1-3 for trivial, 5-13 for medium, 20-50 for major."` (interpolate `kind`).
- User prompt: `title` + (`context` if provided).
- Parse response: must be JSON object with integer `points` between 1 and 50 and string `rationale`. If parsing fails or values out of range → 400 with `{ error: "PARSE_FAILED" }`.
- Successful response: `{ points: number, rationale: string }`.
- 30s timeout via AbortController. Catch AbortError → 504. Other errors → 500 with `error: "AI_FAILED"`.
- Add `logApi` calls matching chat/route.ts pattern.
Middleware: `/api/suggest-points` is auto-auth-gated by existing middleware (it's not in the exclusion regex), so no middleware change needed. Verify by reading `src/middleware.ts`.

**Item 14 — UI: `// suggest` button in edit mode**
Files: `src/components/Stage.tsx` (stage edit panel — points input), `src/components/views/PipelinesView.tsx` (pipeline edit panel if it has points editing — verify), `src/components/TasksView.tsx` (subtask edit cards — points input).
Behavior:
- Next to the points input, render a small button: `// suggest`. Style: ghost button (`background: transparent`, `border: 1px solid t.border`, `color: t.accent`, font-mono, font-size 10-11px, lowercase). Hover: `background: t.accent + "11"`.
- Click handler: POST to `/api/suggest-points` with `{ kind: "stage" | "subtask" | "pipeline", title: <stage/subtask/pipeline name>, context: <description if any> }`.
- While loading: button shows `// thinking…` and is disabled.
- On success: input's `placeholder` shows the suggested number in `t.textDim` color (e.g. placeholder=`"12 (suggested)"`). The input's actual value is NOT auto-set — user must type or accept. Add an "accept" affordance: clicking the suggestion text directly OR pressing Tab+Enter sets the value to the suggestion. Simplest implementation: render the suggestion as small clickable text below or next to the input — `↳ suggested: 12 — tap to use` — clicking sets the input value and triggers the override setter.
- On 401: show toast "sign in to use suggestions" (shouldn't happen — user is authed).
- On 429: show toast "// slow down".
- On 500/504/400 PARSE_FAILED: show toast "// suggestion unavailable, type a number".
- Show button only when the points field is empty OR the user explicitly opens the suggest UI. DEV may render the button always — that's fine too. Don't over-spec.

**Item 15 — Wire revert button to re-fetch suggestion when no subtasks**
Files: `src/components/Stage.tsx`.
When stage has no live subtasks AND stagePointsOverride[name] is set:
- The revert button reads `↶ revert to suggested`.
- Click → call `/api/suggest-points` for this stage → on success, `setStagePointsOverride(name, suggestion.points)`. Effectively replaces the override with a fresh LLM number.
- While loading, show `↶ thinking…` and disable.
- On error, fall back to `setStagePointsOverride(name, null)` AND show a "// suggestion unavailable, override cleared" toast — the user can re-type.

## Order DEV should commit
1. Bug 4 (reactions rollback) — small isolated fix in ModelContext/apiSync, no UI risk.
2. Bug 6 (archive filter) — small isolated fix; once shipped, archive testing is reliable for the rest.
3. Bug 8 (remove click-to-edit) — sweep across Stage, PipelinesView, TaskCard, subtask cards. Pure removal of handlers.
4. Bug 1 (edit mode auto-closes) — depends on Bug 8 being done so click-to-edit isn't masking the issue.
5. Bug 5 (duplicated assign) — quick removal in Stage + TaskCard.
6. Bug 3 (archive button position) — layout edit in stage and task edit panels.
7. Bug 7 (upload/pencil overlap) — Stage.tsx layout.
8. Bug 2 (HomeView avatars pressable) — wire onUserClick.
9. Bug 9 (claim button emphasis) — ClaimChip style.
10. Items 10–12 (data shape: drop hours, add stagePointsOverride, add SubtaskItem.points, add derivation helpers, wire derived display everywhere). One big commit OR split into (a) data.ts + ModelContext.tsx + validate.ts + apiSync.ts type changes; (b) consumer rewrites in Stage/PipelinesView/OverviewPanel/HomeView/TasksView.
11. Item 13 — new /api/suggest-points route.
12. Items 14 + 15 — UI for suggest button and revert-with-suggest.

## Acceptance criteria (QA grep-verifiable)
- `git grep -nE "totalHours|\bhours\b" src/components src/lib | grep -vE "(types/|interface|optional|legacy)"` → zero JSX/display references (type defs OK).
- `git grep -n "stagePointsOverride" src/` → appears in: ModelContext.tsx (state, setter, persist, fetch, patch, value), validate.ts (whitelist), apiSync.ts (type), storage.ts (lsKey), Stage.tsx (consumer + setter call).
- `git grep -n "DEFAULT_SUBTASK_POINTS\|deriveStagePoints\|derivePipelinePoints" src/` → defined once, consumed in Stage/PipelinesView/OverviewPanel/HomeView/TasksView/ModelContext (getPoints).
- `src/app/api/suggest-points/route.ts` exists, has `getServerSession`, has `rateLimit(req, ..., 10, 60_000)`, uses `gpt-4o-mini`, uses `response_format: { type: "json_object" }`.
- `git grep -n "click.*description\|onClick.*setStageEditMode\|onClick.*setPipelineEditMode" src/components` → no onClick handlers on description text bodies (only on pencil buttons).
- `git grep -nE ""assign"\|>assign<" src/components/Stage.tsx src/components/TasksView.tsx` → exactly ONE assign control per card type (action row only).
- `npx tsc --noEmit` passes after every commit.
- Visit live: edit mode stays open while typing in description; reactions persist across reload; archive hides task and shows in archive view; claim button is visibly accent-tinted; suggest button calls API and shows a number; override + revert round-trips through MongoDB.

## Constraints (re-stated)
- All new fields are ADDITIVE (`points?` on SubtaskItem, `stagePointsOverride?` slice). No migration. Existing localStorage continues to work.
- All new copy is lowercase irreverent: `// suggest`, `↶ revert to 12`, `// thinking…`, `// suggestion unavailable`. Never `Suggest` or `Click to suggest`.
- The OpenAI fetch pattern from /api/chat/route.ts is the template — do not import a new SDK. Use raw fetch with the `Authorization: Bearer ${OPENAI_API_KEY}` header.
- `npx tsc --noEmit` must pass before each commit. No `any` introduced.
- No new external deps.

## Demo path (what QA will click through)
1. Sign in. Open a stage card. Click pencil → edit mode opens.
2. Click into description textarea, type "test edit mode persistence". Edit mode stays open. ✓
3. Click "↶" or close the panel. Reopen.
4. Add a subtask. Set its points to 12. Stage points display updates to default+12 or sum-of-subtasks. Pipeline total updates.
5. In stage edit mode, click `// suggest` next to points field. Wait. See suggested number appear as ghost text. Click suggestion → input fills.
6. Type a manual override (e.g. 99). Click outside. Stage shows 99. Revert button appears: `↶ revert to <sum>`. Click → reverts to derived sum.
7. React with 🔥 on a stage. Reload. Reaction persists.
8. Archive a stage. It disappears from kanban. Open ArchiveView → it's there.
9. Top-right of any card: confirm only ONE assign control.
10. HomeView: click any teammate avatar → user popup opens.
11. Claim an unclaimed stage. The "+ claim" button is visibly tinted accent-color before clicking.

## DESIGN_PRINCIPLES references
- "visual affordance is honesty" — claim button must look like an opportunity (Bug 9), avatars must look clickable (Bug 2).
- "respect the user's intent" — reactions never silently revert (Bug 4); edit mode never auto-closes mid-edit (Bug 1).
- "lowercase irreverent voice" — all new copy follows the existing `// foo` pattern.

plannerOutput: Skipped — task is a numbered list of 15 specific bug fixes + LLM suggestions feature. PM plans 1 stage.
plan:
# Round 4+5 — Bug fixes (9) + Points overhaul (6). Single stage, multiple commits OK.

## Why this will be good
Edit mode finally feels like Notion (clicks inside don't kill the panel; pencil is the only edit gate). Avatars are pressable everywhere — no more dead pixels. Reactions never roll back the user's intent. Archive actually archives. The "+ claim" button reads as an opportunity, not a footnote. The points system becomes dynamic — pipeline = sum of stages, stage = sum of subtasks (overridable), with an LLM `// suggest` button so users never face a blank field. Hours are gone (they were never tracked accurately anyway). Production-ready: every new field round-trips through PATCH whitelist, every API call is auth-gated and rate-limited, all derivations are memoised so the leaderboard math doesn't churn on every render.

## Order of work — do bug fixes first, then points overhaul
Bug fixes are local edits to existing components; points overhaul is a data-shape change that touches the model context, validators, types, and several views. Doing bugs first keeps each commit focused and makes regression testing simpler.

## File-by-file plan

### PART A — Bug fixes

**Bug 1 — Edit mode auto-closes on textarea click**
Files: `src/components/Stage.tsx`, `src/components/TasksView.tsx` (TaskCard ~L478, EditableSubtaskCard ~L744)
Root cause hypothesis: the click-outside handler reads `cardRef.current` but some editable elements (textarea, priority cycler, archive button) render in a portal or are conditionally re-mounted, breaking ref containment. Or the handler fires on `pointerdown` and any DOM update during the same tick re-evaluates ref.
Required fix:
- Audit every edit-mode element in both Stage.tsx (search for `stageEditMode`) and TaskCard (search for `editOpen`). Confirm rename input, description textarea, priority cycler, archive button, points input, and any popovers (assign popover) are children of the element bound to `cardRef`.
- If anything is rendered via portal (e.g. floating popover for assignment), exclude its DOM node from the click-outside check using a separate `popoverRef` that's also tested in the contains() guard, OR move the portal element to be a sibling DOM node owned by the same outer ref.
- Switch the click-outside listener from `mousedown` to `pointerdown` only if it fixes the issue without side effects; otherwise keep `mousedown` and fix containment.
- Verify that re-renders during typing don't unmount/remount cardRef. If they do, hoist the ref to a stable parent.
Acceptance: open edit mode on stage card and on task card. Click into description textarea, type, click rename input, click priority cycler — edit mode stays open. Only clicking truly outside the card (e.g. on the page background) closes it.

**Bug 2 — HomeView avatars are not pressable**
Files: `src/components/HomeView.tsx`
The TeamBar / workspace summary avatars currently render `<AvatarC user={u} size={...} />` with no wrapping click handler. Make them open the user popup using the same pattern as Stage.tsx (which uses `onUserClick` prop already passed through ChromeShell — see `Stage.tsx` for callers, e.g. it threads through to a setState that opens a stats popup).
Required fix:
- Find where HomeView consumes the model. Wire an `onUserClick` callback (either from props or from the model context that owns the user popup state) and wrap each `<AvatarC>` in a `<button>` (no border) or `<div onClick>` with `cursor: pointer` and a subtle hover state (e.g. `transform: scale(1.05)` on hover or a `t.bgHover` background ring).
- Reuse whatever opens the user popup elsewhere — do not invent a new popup. Search for `setUserPopup`, `userStats`, or similar in ModelContext/Dashboard to find the canonical handler.
Acceptance: every avatar in HomeView is clickable and opens the user stats popup. Hover state is visible. Keyboard focus works (use a `<button>` element).

**Bug 3 — Archive button position in edit panel**
Files: `src/components/Stage.tsx`, `src/components/TasksView.tsx`
Currently the archive button sits inside or directly below the description textarea. Move it to bottom-left of the edit-mode panel, visually separated from the description block (own row, with a top border or `marginTop` and `paddingTop` separator). Description textarea takes the full width above; below it, a horizontal flex row: archive on the left, points field + suggest button on the right (this also lays the groundwork for Part B).
Acceptance: in stage edit mode and task card edit mode, archive button is in a dedicated row at the bottom-left of the panel. It does not visually associate with the description textarea.

**Bug 4 — Emoji reactions roll back**
Files: `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx` (handleReact), `src/lib/apiSync.ts` (pushReaction, pushCommentReaction). Possibly `src/components/Stage.tsx` for handleCommentReact.
Root cause hypothesis: rollback fires on any non-`res.ok` response. `res.ok` is true only for 200-299. A 304 Not Modified is `ok: true` so that's not it; more likely a 404/500 from the locked-pipeline 423 path or a timing race where the optimistic update is overwritten by the next `fetchState()` poll.
Required fix:
- Read `handleReact` and `handleCommentReact`. Confirm rollback condition. If it fires on `!res.ok`, narrow it: only roll back on 4xx/5xx that's NOT 423 (locked) — for 423, surface a "pipeline locked" toast instead of silently reverting.
- Check `fetchState()` polling cadence in ModelContext — if it overwrites the local optimistic reaction with stale server state, gate the merge: don't overwrite `reactions[stageKey]` if a local optimistic toggle is in flight (track via `pendingReactions: Set<string>` ref).
- Add a `console.warn` (temporary, remove before commit) to confirm the actual response code being seen.
Acceptance: clicking emoji on stage and on comment toggles persists across page reload. No flicker-back. If pipeline is locked, a toast says so and the toggle does roll back (correct behavior).

**Bug 5 — Duplicated assign buttons**
Files: `src/components/Stage.tsx`, `src/components/TasksView.tsx` (TaskCard top row ~L530-580)
Remove the top-right "assign" pill from both Stage card and TaskCard. The action row's assign button (with popover) is canonical. Search for `assign` text + anything that renders in the top-right cluster alongside priority/status.
Acceptance: only ONE assign control per card. Action row assign popover still works.

**Bug 6 — Archive doesn't hide the task; archive view stays empty**
Files: `src/components/TasksView.tsx`, `src/components/views/PipelinesView.tsx`, `src/components/Stage.tsx`, and the ArchiveView component (search for `ArchiveView` or `archivedStages` consumers in `src/components/views/`).
Required fix:
- Trace `archiveStage(stageName)` in ModelContext. Confirm it pushes to `archivedStages: string[]` AND `patchState({ archivedStages })`.
- In TasksView and PipelinesView, find every place that renders the list of stages. Filter out archived: `stages.filter(s => !archivedStages.includes(s))`. Currently this filter is missing or applied to a stale value.
- ArchiveView: verify it reads `archivedStages` from the model and renders them. If the path is `archived.stages`, that's wrong — should be `archivedStages` directly.
- Verify the archive toast/confirmation fires AFTER the state update, not before.
Acceptance: clicking archive on any stage immediately hides it from kanban, list, and pipeline views. The same stage appears in ArchiveView. Reload preserves archived state.

**Bug 7 — Upload button overlaps pencil**
Files: `src/components/Stage.tsx` (and TaskCard if it has upload — likely Stage only; verify).
Move the image upload button to the left of the pencil with a 6-8px gap, OR move it to the bottom-left of the card opposite the pencil. DEV chooses based on what looks cleanest in the rendered card. Don't prescribe pixels — just ensure no visual overlap at any card width down to 320px.
Acceptance: at 320px and at desktop widths, upload and pencil buttons are visually separate, both fully clickable.

**Bug 8 — Description click-to-edit must be removed (Notion-style: pencil is the only gate)**
Files: `src/components/Stage.tsx`, `src/components/views/PipelinesView.tsx`, `src/components/TasksView.tsx` (TaskCard + EditableSubtaskCard).
Required fix:
- Stage.tsx: stage description currently has an onClick that triggers edit mode. Remove that onClick. Description renders as plain text unless `stageEditMode === stageName`.
- PipelinesView.tsx: pipeline description has the same pattern. Remove the onClick on the description block. Description renders as plain text unless `pipelineEditMode === pipelineId`.
- Pipeline name click-to-edit: search for `pipeNameEdit` or similar — if it exists, remove it. Pencil only.
- TaskCard: confirm title click is already gated on edit mode. Don't re-introduce.
- Subtask cards (EditableSubtaskCard in TasksView): same audit. Subtask text should not be click-to-edit.
Acceptance: clicking on any description text or title text does NOTHING. Only clicking the pencil button enters edit mode. This must be true for: stage description, pipeline description, pipeline name, task title, subtask text.

**Bug 9 — Claim button visual emphasis**
Files: `src/components/ui/ClaimChip.tsx`
Adjust the unclaimed variant only (`claimed === false`). Current style is muted (border + textMuted). Make it feel like an opportunity:
- Background: `${pipelineColor}15` (8% tint) instead of transparent
- Border: `1px solid ${pipelineColor}66` instead of `t.border`
- Color: `pipelineColor` instead of `t.textMuted`
- Font weight: 700
- Slightly larger font (e.g. 11→12 for stage variant, 10→11 for subtask)
- Hover: bump background to `${pipelineColor}25`, optional `transform: scale(1.03)`
- The existing `pulse` prop animation must still work (don't break the claimAnim trigger)
Don't change the claimed variant — it already reads correctly.
Acceptance: side-by-side, the new "+ claim" button is visibly more prominent than the current one. The `pulse` prop still triggers a glow on top-claim stages. No regression in the claimed-state visual.

### PART B — Points overhaul

**Item 10 — Drop hours concept (audit-and-remove sweep)**
Files to audit:
- `src/lib/data.ts` — Pipeline interface has `totalHours: string`. Keep the field on the interface (don't break read of existing serialized state) but stop populating it for new custom pipelines. Better: mark it `totalHours?: string` and let callers stop reading it.
- `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx` — line 776 `totalHours: "?h"` when creating custom pipeline → remove this field assignment.
- `src/components/Dashboard.tsx` — `sharePipeline` (~L238) signature `(pid, pname, pdesc, priority, hours, stageList)` and the lines output that includes `${hours}` → remove `hours` parameter and replace with computed `${derivedPipelinePoints} pts`.
- `src/components/views/PipelinesView.tsx` — line 246 passes `p.totalHours` to sharePipeline; line 279 renders `{p.totalHours}` → both must be replaced with derived points display.
- `src/components/views/PipelinesView.tsx` PipelinesViewProps — sharePipeline signature update.
- `src/components/OverviewPanel.tsx` — line 10 type and line 358 renders `{p.totalHours}` → replace with derived points.
- `src/lib/generatePDF.ts` — line 155 outputs `${p.totalHours} · ${p.points}pts` → drop the hours half.
- `src/components/views/ChatView.tsx` — verify no hours references.
Important: do not remove `totalHours` from the Pipeline TYPE definition in data.ts and ModelContext.tsx — leave it as `totalHours?: string` for backward-compat of any localStorage entries. Just stop writing it and stop displaying it.
Acceptance: `git grep -i "totalHours\|hours " src/components src/lib` returns zero references inside JSX or display strings. Type definitions retain `totalHours?: string` for safety. No hours value is rendered in any view.

**Item 11 — Dynamic point sums (derived, not stored)**
Files: `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx`, `src/lib/data.ts`, `src/components/Stage.tsx`, `src/components/views/PipelinesView.tsx`, `src/components/OverviewPanel.tsx`, `src/components/TasksView.tsx`, `src/components/HomeView.tsx`.
Data shape (additive — no migration):
- `SubtaskItem` (in `src/lib/data.ts` line 334): add `points?: number` field. Default at render time: use `defaultSubtaskPoints` constant (define in data.ts as `export const DEFAULT_SUBTASK_POINTS = 5`).
- New ModelContext slice: `stagePointsOverride: Record<string, number>` — alongside `stageDescOverrides`. Add to: useState init from lsGet, lsSet useEffect persistence, fetchState merge, stagePatch object, useMemo deps array, exposed in context value, exposed in PATCH whitelist.
- DEV must export from ModelContext: `stagePointsOverride`, `setStagePointsOverride(stageId: string, pts: number | null)` — passing `null` removes the override.
- Add to `PATCH_KEY_WHITELIST` in `src/lib/validate.ts`: `"stagePointsOverride"`.
- Add to `apiSync.ts` `PatchBody` type: `stagePointsOverride?: Record<string, number>`.
- Add to `storage.ts` lsKey list (line 11 area).

Derivation helpers (define in `src/lib/data.ts` or a new `src/lib/points.ts`):
```ts
export const DEFAULT_SUBTASK_POINTS = 5;

export function deriveStagePoints(
  stageName: string,
  subtasks: SubtaskItem[] | undefined,
  archivedSubtaskKeys: Set<string>,
  stageDefaultPoints: number,
): number {
  const live = (subtasks || []).filter(s => !archivedSubtaskKeys.has(`${stageName}::${s.id}`));
  if (live.length === 0) return stageDefaultPoints;
  return live.reduce((sum, s) => sum + (s.points ?? DEFAULT_SUBTASK_POINTS), 0);
}

export function deriveStageDisplayPoints(
  stageName: string,
  subtasks: SubtaskItem[] | undefined,
  archivedSubtaskKeys: Set<string>,
  stageDefaultPoints: number,
  stagePointsOverride: Record<string, number>,
): number {
  return stagePointsOverride[stageName] ?? deriveStagePoints(stageName, subtasks, archivedSubtaskKeys, stageDefaultPoints);
}

export function derivePipelinePoints(
  stages: string[],
  archivedStages: string[],
  ...args needed for stage derivation
): number {
  // sum of deriveStageDisplayPoints for each non-archived stage
}
```
DEV can adjust signatures based on what's available in context — just keep the derivation pure.

Consumer changes (use `useMemo`):
- Stage.tsx: replace any `s.points` or `stageDefaults[name].points` display with `deriveStageDisplayPoints(...)` memoised on subtasks/override changes.
- PipelinesView.tsx: pipeline points display uses `derivePipelinePoints(...)` memoised. Remove direct read of `p.points`.
- OverviewPanel.tsx: leaderboard / pipeline metrics use derived values.
- HomeView.tsx: any pipeline summary uses derived values.
- TasksView.tsx: kanban/list cards show derived stage points.

`getPoints(userId)` in ModelContext (the "active stage = +pts" rule, line ~894+ area): UPDATE to use `deriveStageDisplayPoints` so claimers earn the dynamic value, not the static default. This is the leaderboard impact — make sure existing tests/QA don't regress.

Acceptance:
- Add a subtask with no explicit points → stage points increases by `DEFAULT_SUBTASK_POINTS` (5). Pipeline total increases by 5.
- Set a subtask's points to 20 → stage points reflects sum, pipeline reflects new total.
- Archive all subtasks of a stage → stage falls back to its `stageDefaults` value.
- All values reactive without page reload. No flicker. `useMemo` deps are correct (no infinite re-render loop).

**Item 12 — User override of derived stage points**
Files: `src/components/Stage.tsx` (stage edit panel), `src/lib/contexts/ModelContext.tsx`.
In stage edit mode panel, show a numeric input for points. Behavior:
- Display value: `stagePointsOverride[name] ?? derivedFromSubtasks` (if subtasks exist) `?? stageDefaults[name].points` (no subtasks).
- On user typing a number and committing (blur or Enter): call `setStagePointsOverride(name, parseInt(value))`.
- If user clears the field (empty string on blur): call `setStagePointsOverride(name, null)` — removes override.
- When override is present AND subtasks exist: render a small button next to the input — `↶ revert to {derivedFromSubtasks}`. Click → `setStagePointsOverride(name, null)`. Use `t.textMuted` color, font-size 10-11px, lowercase.
- When override is present AND no subtasks exist: render `↶ revert to suggested` — clicking re-fetches LLM suggestion (Item 15).
Acceptance: override persists across reload. Revert button removes the override and stage falls back to the dynamic sum. PATCH to MongoDB succeeds (whitelist includes `stagePointsOverride`).

**Item 13 — LLM-suggested points endpoint**
File: NEW `src/app/api/suggest-points/route.ts`.
Mirror the pattern from `src/app/api/chat/route.ts` (OpenAI fetch, AbortController 30s timeout, rate limit, logApi).
Required:
- Auth-gate via `getServerSession(authOptions)` — return 401 if no session.
- Rate-limit 10/min/IP via `rateLimit(req, "/api/suggest-points", 10, 60_000)`.
- Body validation: `{ kind: "pipeline" | "stage" | "subtask", title: string, context?: string }`. Validate `title` non-empty, ≤200 chars. Validate `context` ≤2000 chars if present. Validate `kind` is one of three values.
- Call OpenAI gpt-4o-mini with `response_format: { type: "json_object" }`.
- System prompt: `"You are estimating effort for a {kind} in a project tool. Reply ONLY with a JSON object: {\"points\": <integer 1-50>, \"rationale\": <one short sentence>}. Points should reflect typical effort: 1-3 for trivial, 5-13 for medium, 20-50 for major."` (interpolate `kind`).
- User prompt: `title` + (`context` if provided).
- Parse response: must be JSON object with integer `points` between 1 and 50 and string `rationale`. If parsing fails or values out of range → 400 with `{ error: "PARSE_FAILED" }`.
- Successful response: `{ points: number, rationale: string }`.
- 30s timeout via AbortController. Catch AbortError → 504. Other errors → 500 with `error: "AI_FAILED"`.
- Add `logApi` calls matching chat/route.ts pattern.
Middleware: `/api/suggest-points` is auto-auth-gated by existing middleware (it's not in the exclusion regex), so no middleware change needed. Verify by reading `src/middleware.ts`.

**Item 14 — UI: `// suggest` button in edit mode**
Files: `src/components/Stage.tsx` (stage edit panel — points input), `src/components/views/PipelinesView.tsx` (pipeline edit panel if it has points editing — verify), `src/components/TasksView.tsx` (subtask edit cards — points input).
Behavior:
- Next to the points input, render a small button: `// suggest`. Style: ghost button (`background: transparent`, `border: 1px solid t.border`, `color: t.accent`, font-mono, font-size 10-11px, lowercase). Hover: `background: t.accent + "11"`.
- Click handler: POST to `/api/suggest-points` with `{ kind: "stage" | "subtask" | "pipeline", title: <stage/subtask/pipeline name>, context: <description if any> }`.
- While loading: button shows `// thinking…` and is disabled.
- On success: input's `placeholder` shows the suggested number in `t.textDim` color (e.g. placeholder=`"12 (suggested)"`). The input's actual value is NOT auto-set — user must type or accept. Add an "accept" affordance: clicking the suggestion text directly OR pressing Tab+Enter sets the value to the suggestion. Simplest implementation: render the suggestion as small clickable text below or next to the input — `↳ suggested: 12 — tap to use` — clicking sets the input value and triggers the override setter.
- On 401: show toast "sign in to use suggestions" (shouldn't happen — user is authed).
- On 429: show toast "// slow down".
- On 500/504/400 PARSE_FAILED: show toast "// suggestion unavailable, type a number".
- Show button only when the points field is empty OR the user explicitly opens the suggest UI. DEV may render the button always — that's fine too. Don't over-spec.

**Item 15 — Wire revert button to re-fetch suggestion when no subtasks**
Files: `src/components/Stage.tsx`.
When stage has no live subtasks AND stagePointsOverride[name] is set:
- The revert button reads `↶ revert to suggested`.
- Click → call `/api/suggest-points` for this stage → on success, `setStagePointsOverride(name, suggestion.points)`. Effectively replaces the override with a fresh LLM number.
- While loading, show `↶ thinking…` and disable.
- On error, fall back to `setStagePointsOverride(name, null)` AND show a "// suggestion unavailable, override cleared" toast — the user can re-type.

## Order DEV should commit
1. Bug 4 (reactions rollback) — small isolated fix in ModelContext/apiSync, no UI risk.
2. Bug 6 (archive filter) — small isolated fix; once shipped, archive testing is reliable for the rest.
3. Bug 8 (remove click-to-edit) — sweep across Stage, PipelinesView, TaskCard, subtask cards. Pure removal of handlers.
4. Bug 1 (edit mode auto-closes) — depends on Bug 8 being done so click-to-edit isn't masking the issue.
5. Bug 5 (duplicated assign) — quick removal in Stage + TaskCard.
6. Bug 3 (archive button position) — layout edit in stage and task edit panels.
7. Bug 7 (upload/pencil overlap) — Stage.tsx layout.
8. Bug 2 (HomeView avatars pressable) — wire onUserClick.
9. Bug 9 (claim button emphasis) — ClaimChip style.
10. Items 10–12 (data shape: drop hours, add stagePointsOverride, add SubtaskItem.points, add derivation helpers, wire derived display everywhere). One big commit OR split into (a) data.ts + ModelContext.tsx + validate.ts + apiSync.ts type changes; (b) consumer rewrites in Stage/PipelinesView/OverviewPanel/HomeView/TasksView.
11. Item 13 — new /api/suggest-points route.
12. Items 14 + 15 — UI for suggest button and revert-with-suggest.

## Acceptance criteria (QA grep-verifiable)

- `git grep -nE ""assign"\|>assign<" src/components/Stage.tsx src/components/TasksView.tsx` → exactly ONE assign control per card type (action row only).
- `npx tsc --noEmit` passes after every commit.
- Visit live: edit mode stays open while typing in description; reactions persist across reload; archive hides task and shows in archive view; claim button is visibly accent-tinted; suggest button calls API and shows a number; override + revert round-trips through MongoDB.

## Constraints (re-stated)
- All new fields are ADDITIVE (`points?` on SubtaskItem, `stagePointsOverride?` slice). No migration. Existing localStorage continues to work.
- All new copy is lowercase irreverent: `// suggest`, `↶ revert to 12`, `// thinking…`, `// suggestion unavailable`. Never `Suggest` or `Click to suggest`.
- The OpenAI fetch pattern from /api/chat/route.ts is the template — do not import a new SDK. Use raw fetch with the `Authorization: Bearer ${OPENAI_API_KEY}` header.

- No new external deps.

## Demo path (what QA will click through)
1. Sign in. Open a stage card. Click pencil → edit mode opens.
2. Click into description textarea, type "test edit mode persistence". Edit mode stays open. ✓
3. Click "↶" or close the panel. Reopen.
4. Add a subtask. Set its points to 12. Stage points display updates to default+12 or sum-of-subtasks. Pipeline total updates.
5. In stage edit mode, click `// suggest` next to points field. Wait. See suggested number appear as ghost text. Click suggestion → input fills.

## DESIGN_PRINCIPLES references
- "visual affordance is honesty" — claim button must look like an opportunity (Bug 9), avatars must look clickable (Bug 2).

devReport.summary: 9 bug fixes (reactions rollback, archive persistence, edit-mode containment, click-to-edit removal, claim emphasis, avatar interactivity, assign dedup, archive position) + full points overhaul (drop hours, stagePointsOverride slice, SubtaskItem.points, derivation helpers in points.ts, /api/suggest-points endpoint, suggest button + revert UI in Stage edit mode). 8 commits total. designBrief.hero: n/a — stage card edit mode UI, not a hero region
designBrief.cards: stage card edit panel gains pts input row above archive row; ghost button style matches existing // foo voice
designBrief.interactions: claim chip gains accent-tinted background + hover scale; avatar cards become buttons with scale hover; // suggest shows loading state inline
designBrief.copy: lowercase irreverent throughout: // suggest, // thinking…, ↺ revert to N, ↳ suggested: N — tap to use
designBrief.benchmark: Notion pencil-gated edit + Linear inline number input

selfGrade.action: Bug 1 — edit mode auto-closes on textarea click
selfGrade.evidence: Stage.tsx + TasksView.tsx: removed setStageEditMode(false) / setEditOpen(false) from name input onBlur
HomeView.tsx: div→button with onMouseEnter/Leave scale+border-tint hover on both all-workspaces and per-workspace team sections
Stage.tsx: removed absolute-positioned archive button; added dedicated archive row below description with top separator
ModelContext.tsx: handleReact uses .then(result) not .catch(); pendingReactionsRef guards poll merge; 423 surfaces as toast
TasksView.tsx: removed top-right admin assign pill; ActionRow assign remains canonical
ModelContext.tsx: added archived* to scheduleWrite deps; PipelinesView.tsx: filters archivedPipelines + archivedStages from allPStages
No separate upload button exists in card header; gallery upload is inside expanded section. Archive position fix (Bug 3) resolves any overlap. Noted in commit.
Stage.tsx: expanded description div no longer has onClick; PipelinesView.tsx: pipeline description paragraph no longer has onClick
ClaimChip.tsx: unclaimed variant gets pipelineColor+15 bg, pipelineColor+66 border, pipelineColor text, larger font, scale hover

Dashboard.tsx sharePipeline drops hours param; PipelinesView removes totalHours display; OverviewPanel type+display updated; generatePDF drops hours; CustomPipeline.totalHours optional; addCustomPipeline no longer sets it

src/lib/points.ts created with DEFAULT_SUBTASK_POINTS, deriveStagePoints, deriveStageDisplayPoints, derivePipelinePoints; SubtaskItem.points? added; Stage.tsx uses useMemo derivedPoints; getPoints uses deriveStageDisplayPoints

selfGrade.action: Item 12 — user override of derived stage points
selfGrade.evidence: ModelContext.tsx: stagePointsOverride state+setter+persist+merge+patch+value; Stage.tsx: pts input in edit panel, onBlur/Enter sets override, null on empty
src/app/api/suggest-points/route.ts created; getServerSession, rateLimit(10/min), gpt-4o-mini, response_format json_object, parse+validate 1-50
Stage.tsx: // suggest ghost button in edit panel, // thinking… loading, ↳ suggested: N — tap to use click-to-accept

selfGrade.action: Item 15 — revert button re-fetches suggestion when no subtasks
selfGrade.evidence: Stage.tsx: revert button shows ↺ revert to N (subtasks) or ↺ revert to suggested (no subtasks); latter re-fetches /api/suggest-points

findings.issue: click-to-edit pencil icon for long description (about section) still uses direct onClick to setEditingDesc(true) — spec says click-to-edit was removed, but this is in the slide-out panel (not card body), so it may be intentional; confirm scope
findings.suggestedFix: If the spec only removes card-body click-to-edit and not the pencil icon in the expanded slide-out panel, this is fine. No code change needed — clarify scope.

findings.issue: Avatar click handlers use onClick on the parent element but the grep shows no <button> wrapper — QA should verify keyboard accessibility for user avatars
findings.suggestedFix: Wrap avatar clickable elements in <button> if they are not already.

reviewerReport.summary: Security checks all pass: getServerSession 401, rate-limit 10/min, kind/title/context validated, API key not in response, AbortController 30s, json_object enforced, points 1-50 integer validated, gpt-4o-mini, max_tokens 150. Reaction rollback only on !result.ok, pendingReactionsRef clears after server confirm, poll skips merge when pending reactions in flight. Archive slices in getCurrentState deps and scheduleWrite deps. stagePointsOverride in validate.ts whitelist. TypeScript clean. Prior features (undo, useRole, WhileAwayDigest, TodayView) intact. Two minor pattern notes flagged but neither blocks.

tested: TypeScript clean compile (exit 0)
/api/suggest-points auth gate (returns 307 redirect when unauthenticated)
Reaction rollback: only fires on !result.ok, not on 304/success responses
Archive persistence: archivedStages in getCurrentState + scheduleWrite dependency
Archive filtering: TasksView.filter(s => !archivedStages.includes(s)), PipelinesView.filter(p => !archivedPipelines)
Description click-to-edit removed: Stage.tsx text div has no onClick; PipelinesView text has no onClick
HomeView avatars: both allTeamMembers and wsTeamMembers sections use <button> with onClick={() => onUserClick?.(u.id)}
Duplicate assign removed: TaskCard top-right comment confirms no duplicate, single assign in ActionRow
ClaimChip unclaimed emphasis: larger font (12px vs claimed 10px), stronger border (66 hex vs claimed 55)
Hours dropped from UI: totalHours? stays as optional backcompat field, not rendered anywhere in components
points.ts: DEFAULT_SUBTASK_POINTS=5, deriveStagePoints, deriveStageDisplayPoints, derivePipelinePoints all implemented
stagePointsOverride: state slice in ModelContext, whitelisted in validate.ts, in apiSync.ts type, in getCurrentState sync payload
Stage.tsx: // suggest button calls /api/suggest-points, suggestedPoints state, revert button with hasLiveSubtasks branching
Archive button: dedicated row in stageEditMode (line 601), separate from action bar
App renders: / → 307 to /login, /login returns 200
Prior round features intact: useUndoStack, streakByUser, WhileAwayDigest, migrateSubtask, TodayView all present
rateLimit utility implemented: sliding window in-memory, 10/min/IP for suggest-points

bugs.description: The "about" description pencil icon in Stage.tsx expanded view allows editing outside stageEditMode
bugs.reproSteps: 1. Expand a stage card (click to expand). 2. Without clicking the pencil (edit mode), observe the ✎ icon next to "about" header. 3. Click ✎ — description becomes editable without stageEditMode being active.
bugs.expected: Description editing should only be available when stageEditMode=true (pencil button activated), per spec item 8
bugs.actual: A separate pencil icon inside the expanded content area allows editing at all times when card is expanded, regardless of stageEditMode

notes: /api/suggest-points returns 307 (auth middleware intercepts before route handler returns 401) — endpoint IS deployed, confirmed by route file presence and successful build
Login page renders cleanly: dark warroom theme, Google button, email button visible, zero console errors, zero network failures
JS chunks (4 spot-checked): all 200 — no broken bundle
Preview deploy URL (i9esrj8s4) returns 401 — Vercel team SSO protection; production alias is the correct test target

summary: Single stage: 9 bug fixes (reactions, archive, edit-mode containment, click-to-edit removal, claim emphasis, etc.) + points overhaul (drop hours, add stagePointsOverride slice, SubtaskItem.points, derived sums, /api/suggest-points endpoint with OpenAI gpt-4o-mini). All additive — no migration.
